data.title: [bug-create workflow]: agents use non-conforming titles when creating bug tickets -> post-creation warning fires but title not fixed
data.description:
## Incident Overview

When agents create bug tickets, they consistently use titles that do not conform to the required [Component]: [Condition] -> [Observed Result] format. The post-creation warning fires ('Bug title does not match recommended pattern') but agents either ignore the warning or fail to call ticket edit to fix the title.

## Observed Pattern

Tickets are created with titles like:
- 'ticket create exits 144 for stories with long --description heredocs' (no brackets, no arrow)
- 'figma_node_mapper.py: effects and layoutMode fields not mapped to spatial-layout.json props' (no [Component] bracket, no arrow)
- 'CI: ShellCheck job fails in main CI' (partial format)

## Expected Behavior

All bug tickets must use: [Component]: [Condition] -> [Observed Result]
Per fix-bug/SKILL.md Step 0.5: 'Post-creation title validation: fix non-conforming titles immediately'

## Root Cause Direction

The title format instruction exists in multiple places (fix-bug SKILL.md, create-bug SKILL.md, debug-everything Validation Mode Step 3) but agents:
1. Do not apply the format at creation time
2. Ignore or fail to parse the post-creation stderr warning
3. The warning message instructs agents to run ticket edit but they skip this step

Fix direction: Strengthen the post-creation validation step in fix-bug SKILL.md and create-bug SKILL.md to be a hard gate (not advisory), and add a concrete example of a conforming title in the prompt shown to agents before ticket creation.
